reasoning: The definition `g n := Real.sqrt (n + 1) - Real.sqrt n` exactly matches $a_i = \sqrt{i+1} - \sqrt{i}$. The Finset expression `∑ i ∈ range n, g i` encodes $\sum_{i<n} a_i$ since `Finset.range n = {0, 1, ..., n-1}`. The filter statement `Tendsto (fun n => ∑ i ∈ range n, g i) atTop atTop` is the standard Lean encoding of "the limit as $n \to \infty$ of the partial sum equals $+\infty$" (i.e., the partial sums are eventually arbitrarily large). There is no drift in hypotheses, types, quantifiers, or conclusion.
